name: Bob Odenkirk
blurb: He co-created and starred in the influential HBO sketch series Mr. Show with Bob and David alongside David Cross.
bio: Bob Odenkirk is an American comedian, writer, and actor who began in Chicago improv and stand-up before becoming an Emmy-winning writer on Saturday Night Live, where he created the Matt Foley motivational-speaker character. He co-created and starred in the influential HBO sketch series Mr. Show with Bob and David alongside David Cross. He briefly pursued stand-up in the mid-1990s but abandoned it, later finding wider fame as Saul Goodman on Breaking Bad and Better Call Saul.

timeline.ev: Hired as a writer on Saturday Night Live
Mr. Show with Bob and David premieres on HBO
Debuts as Saul Goodman on Breaking Bad

accolades: Two-time Primetime Emmy winner for writing (SNL)
Multiple Emmy nominations for Better Call Saul
